programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: A dog arrives at the shelter after being attacked by another animal and left without veterinary treatment

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: Vet Rachel Lee is determined to save an injured dog that has a deep wound on its neck

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Dagues moved to Michigan's wilderness. But they have no power, predators killed their chickens and all their water must be hand-pumped from a well

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: The team members rush to rescue a dog seen on a busy highway. A family travel from Massachusetts hoping to find a four-legged friend to add to their household

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: A pair of cats seemingly intent on destroying their home. Jackson Galaxy helps families calm their unruly felines

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy has his hands full with two cats who have differing behavioural problems - one likes to bully, while the other cannot seem to stop messing

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Wardens Herchman and Stapleton join forces to track down a man who has faked his own death. And a jet skier's life is spared after an explosion

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Shelter volunteers drive from Kansas hoping the team can save a tiny rescue dog. Plus, Dr. Jeff?s cousin asks for the vets to visit Tennessee to provide free care

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Three siblings enlist the help of Pete Nelson and his team in surprising their parents with a rustic art studio treehouse on their historic farm in Wisconsin

programme.title: Blood Ivory
programme.desc: Documentary following Tanzanian rangers as they try to counter the unprecedented growth of the ivory industry, as a result of poachers preying on African elephants

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.sub-title: The Day the Buffalo Broke Free
programme.desc: Tensions flare when Otto and Jane head to Kodiak Island to rescue a herd of wild buffalo. Plus, Shane makes progress on his long-overdue cabin

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns start building their home on their remote land in Alaska. Billy and his four sons venture out to pick up some much-needed lumber and on the way learn of a huge storm coming their way. The Browns must batten down the hatches and take cover, while hoping that their lumber doesn't fall victim to the chaos that large storms create

programme.title: Mountain Monsters
programme.sub-title: The Rogue Team Rises - Part One
programme.desc: Documentary about hunters tracking unidentified creatures. Buck takes action, causing the rogue team to emerge from the shadows after months of silence

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys return to Alaska, but they find their homestead on complete disrepair, so they must race to build a steel tram before the deep freeze sets in again

programme.title: Great White Abyss
programme.desc: A team of experts travels to great white hotspot Guadalupe Island in search of the biggest sharks of their kind, using deep-water cage and submarine dives

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson constructs a thrill-seeker's dream treetop escape in Washington, complete with a bouncy-bridge entryway and a zip-line through the forest

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Stolen cattle are found abandoned beside a road, and officials investigate a case involving a Chihuahua that has been put into storage

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Investigators try to seize seven horses from a waterlogged field, but find the beasts much more reluctant to leave than they expected

programme.title: King Otto and Football's Greek Gods
programme.desc: The story of how Otto Rehhagel coached Greece to become the unlikely winners of Euro 2004 and how two contrasting cultures came together to write a new chapter of Greek mythology. After he accomplished every major success in Germany, Rehhagel made the decision to work in a foreign country with the underachieving Greek national team

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms explores the handsome heroes and pin-ups of the silver screen, including Paul Newman, Robert Redford, Jane Russell and Marilyn Monroe. Plus, hearing from some of the stars themselves talking about what their sex symbol status meant to them

programme.title: Eve and Marilyn
programme.desc: Eve Arnold, one of the world's leading photojournalists, remembers photographing Marilyn Monroe over the 10 crucial years of her stardom and decline. Going back to the original contact sheets and colour material, Arnold tells of their friendship and what it was like as a woman to photograph Marilyn Monroe. First broadcast September 8, 1987

programme.title: Scene by Scene
programme.desc: A rare interview with former Hollywood sex symbol Jane Russell. She talks about her difficult childhood as the eldest and only girl of six siblings, her film career and working with Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The penultimate first-round match of the academic quiz sees four students from Newnham College, Cambridge pitting their wits against a team from the Courtauld Institute of Art in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the first semi-final of the quiz requiring patience and lateral thinking, with the Crossworders taking on the Science Writers for a place in the final

programme.title: India: Nature's Wonderland
programme.desc: Part two of two. Wildlife expert Liz Bonnin, Slumdog Millionaire actress Freida Pinto and mountaineer Jon Gupta explore the natural world in India, including a village of birds, masks that come alive, an impressive mountain range and baby turtles erupting out of the sand

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon concludes his exploration of the artistic history of the northern European region in Sweden - home of IKEA - exploring the country's tradition of furniture design

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Critic Robert Hughes assesses America's contribution to the world of art, beginning by charting the foundation of a national identity that sought architectural inspiration from Greece and Rome, and looks at America's earliest major painters. First broadcast in 1996

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: How the generation of artists who recorded the shocks of global war gave way in the 1950s and 1960s to an explosion of new voices from across the British Isles, reinventing the arts and creating a richer, more diverse culture. Young artists rebelled against the old establishment, kicking against the confines of class, sex, nation and race

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: In the final part of the series, Marilyn triumphs in her most celebrated comedy yet, Some Like It Hot. In the weeks before her death, she is on the cusp of a new and exciting future, making front pages with her portraits, and securing a triumphant new deal with 20th Century Fox. Narrated by Jessica Chastain

programme.title: Being Stanley Baxter
programme.desc: A profile of the Glasgow-born actor, comedian and impressionist, who died in December 2025 at the age of 99, charting his extraordinary professional journey and personal life by drawing on rare archive footage and candid interviews. The film reflects the complex aspects of his long and distinguished life as well as celebrating his comedic genius, particularly his impressionist and satirical work

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the first semi-final of the quiz requiring patience and lateral thinking, with the Crossworders taking on the Science Writers for a place in the final

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: How the generation of artists who recorded the shocks of global war gave way in the 1950s and 1960s to an explosion of new voices from across the British Isles, reinventing the arts and creating a richer, more diverse culture. Young artists rebelled against the old establishment, kicking against the confines of class, sex, nation and race

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The final first-round match of the academic quiz sees Robert Gordon University return to the competition for the first time in 28 years as their team takes on the University of Roehampton, making their debut on the show. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the second semi-final, in which the Bankers face the Lapsed Psychologists for a place in the grand finale

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daddy dresses up in a spaceman suit and goes on the rampage in the toy department of a local shop, forcing an embarrassed Hyacinth to intervene. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy gets out of his depth when helping arrange Frank's wedding. The night before the event, he meets bridesmaid Liz

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama continues his narrative with a look at why Britain proved immune to the ideals of the French Revolution, ignoring the call of liberty, equality and fraternity so readily embraced by the peasantry of France. Instead, the influence of poets, painters and journalists of the Romantic movement persuaded the nation of the primacy of feeling over reason, and divided opinion on the civil strife across the Channel

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Documentary examining the life of the iconic actress. In part one, aspiring star Norma Jeane attends an audition at 20th Century Fox, where the head of casting suggests she change her name to Marilyn Monroe. After connecting with Joseph Schenck, Marilyn is able to get a job at Columbia Pictures, where she receives her first starring role. Narrated by Jessica Chastain

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Marilyn's rise is nearly ruined when news breaks that she posed nude for a calendar earlier in her career. She uses this to her advantage to showcase her body confidence. Monroe also demonstrates her unique talent for comedy in Gentlemen Prefer Blondes, and reaches superstar status

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Marilyn is forging a new identity for herself in New York, but when news breaks that she plans to create her own production company, Darryl Zanuck refuses to let her out of her contract. Narrated by Jessica Chastain

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Helen Czerski explores the science of heat and how the ability to harness it has led to some of humanity's greatest innovations, from steam engines to plasmas. She also reveals how heat is the hidden energy contained within matter, with the power to transform it from one state to another

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the second semi-final, in which the Bankers face the Lapsed Psychologists for a place in the grand finale

programme.title: India: Nature's Wonderland
programme.desc: Part two of two. Wildlife expert Liz Bonnin, Slumdog Millionaire actress Freida Pinto and mountaineer Jon Gupta explore the natural world in India, including a village of birds, masks that come alive, an impressive mountain range and baby turtles erupting out of the sand

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Scarecrows' Wedding
programme.desc: Adaptation of the children's book by Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler, starring Rob Brydon, Jessie Buckley and Domhnall Gleeson. Two devoted scarecrows, Betty O'Barley and Harry O'Hay, plan a wedding to remember but, when Harry insists on leaving the farm to collect one last thing for their big day, a smooth-talking scarecrow named Reginald Rake swoops in with plans of his own. Chaos ensues, and soon Betty's in danger and everything is at risk - can Harry get back in time and save the day?

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Scammers Used Our Street to Open Fake Accounts
programme.desc: The team investigates the fake bank accounts all registered in one Cheshire street, and question if the bank responsible does enough to stop scammers opening accounts

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is charmed by a 19th-century cottage just outside Birmingham, and in particular the old-fashioned phone left behind. There's plenty of room for Tommy Walsh to park at a row of garages in London, and Jacqui Joseph is in Ipswich, Suffolk, where a tiny shower just off the kitchen can't be passed off as being quirky

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Documentary following the actress as she trains as a carer at one of the UK's leading health and social care colleges, where she will study, train, and work alongside students, care professionals, volunteers and home carers. Nerves hit on day one, but her first on-the-job work placement has a real impact

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson spends this break in the company of sporting star Steve Cram. The middle-distance runner and broadcaster joins the host on a boat trip from Seahouses to the Farne Islands to watch seals and seabirds. They also visit Bamburgh beach for a spot of walking meditation

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from urban Hertfordshire with a £550,000 budget have their sights set on moving to the Somerset countryside, where Jules Hudson has some properties lined up to tempt them. However, Jules has a big surprise up his sleeve, as he questions not the type of new home they're looking for but the location of it

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Two green-fingered experts descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Jamie, Liam, Jack and Dan compete for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Rose Matafeo joins Alexander Armstrong for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Documentary following the actress as she trains as a carer at one of the UK's leading health and social care colleges, where she will study, train, and work alongside students, care professionals, volunteers and home carers. Nerves hit on day one, but her first on-the-job work placement has a real impact

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max confronts the Mitchells about his suspicions, Gina gives Eddie the cold shoulder, and Zack gets some unwelcome news

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike and Alison welcome the very first guests to their new bijoux B&B. While serving the couple breakfast, Alison realises that they are not enjoying their break and, fearing a bad review, utilises the surprising skills of Lady Button and Julian to ensure the pair make the most of their weekend away. Thomas is perplexed yet secretly thrilled by the sudden attention from the Plaguers, who, after admiring Alison's discarded portrait of him are now affording him celebrity status in Button House, hanging on to his every word. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Highlights from series 19 of the comedy panel show hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. With appearances by Lucy Beaumont, Bob Mortimer, Jamelia, John Kearns, Harriet Kemsley, Yinka Bokinni, Gyles Brandreth, Dianne Buswell, Mark Chapman, Montell Douglas, Tasha Ghouri, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Will Kirk, David Morrissey, Josh Pugh and Holly Willoughby

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: A breakthrough World Cup reignites belief, and the England team return to a tournament on home turf, amidst the Covid pandemic. As the pressure to win increases, Pippa Grange questions her role. Meanwhile, Gareth tries to unite a divided country ahead of Euro 2020, where he hopes the team finally bring it home at Wembley

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Carl Frampton: On Probation
programme.desc: Carl is back with Probation Officer Toni, who finds out one of her Service Users who is on probation for serious assault is about to become a father again, and puts measures into place to safeguard the progress he has made. Meanwhile in Belfast, Carl meets a woman who successfully completed probation and has big plans for her future, but her struggles with alcohol continue to hang over her

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with guest panellists Phil Wang and Judi Love joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: Arthur the King (2024)
programme.desc: A professional adventure racer looking for one last chance at glory joins a team of athletes on a 435-mile, 10-day event in the Dominican Republic, with the aid of a scrappy stray dog. Fact-based drama, starring Mark Wahlberg, Simu Liu, Juliet Rylance and Nathalie Emmanuel

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Louise Minchin, Julia Somerville and Gloria Hunniford hear the story of an Edinburgh woman left devastated when an online romance turned out to be a scam

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two dog-loving developers tackle epic projects in Wales and Norfolk. A seasoned developer is looking to move quickly and economically in Nottingham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Jacqui Joseph

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: As part of Carers Matter Week, Eric Knowles presents a special episode to celebrate the millions of people who dedicate their lives to helping others. In Newark, Nottinghamshire, Nick Hall and Izzie Balmer help two teams of carers - twin sisters Kayley and Sarah, who both have children with autism and ADHD, and charity worker Mandy and her daughter Jo, whose son has a rare chromosome disorder, similar to cerebral palsy

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie learns about childhood illnesses, meeting up with a friend who talks about her experiences of having a child with cancer and the support her family received from the nursing teams. Natalie then goes on to join a community nursing team, shadowing nurse Charlotte across clinics and home visits, while also facing up to her personal fear of needles

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson is joined by his uncle Matheson as the pair head to the Scottish Borders for a weekend in the outdoors to relax and reset. On their way to their favourite fishing spot in Coldingham, they have a go at hedge laying before Matheson relives his youth riding horses on the beach

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find their first home together in Oxfordshire, with space for a rabbit palace and their impressive collection of house plants. He also visits the University of Oxford Botanic Garden, where he discovers how nature is inspiring exciting new technological developments

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Florence Headlam and Joe Swift are in Stockport to help Sasha and Rick transform their long, muddy, unloved garden into a wild English wonderland. With unsafe concrete steps making the patio hard to access, it's no place for daughter Jocelyn or the new baby on the way, and hosting friends and family is a real challenge. The couple dream of a vibrant, wildlife-friendly space, paired with a practical patio. Can the team deliver everything they want on a £4,000 budget?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends and family Lynne, Derek, John and Kenny compete for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Rose Matafeo on the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant steps up to protect his family, Zack purposely raises suspicions, while Eddie and Harry clash

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The quarter-final sees the teams heading to the literary town of Hay-on-Wye in Wales, where they work in pairs to transform two high street shops. One sells guitars, the other arts and crafts, and the aim of the designers is to improve layout and visual merchandising to entice more customers. Alan follows their progress, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by retail expert Ross Bailey to assess the final results

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Eight famous faces explore their family history, beginning with broadcaster Zoe Ball. With the help of her father, broadcasting favourite Johnny Ball, she uncovers an unexpected connection to Scotland and a tale of hardship and resilience that took the family from industrial 19th-century Glasgow to Bolton. On her late mother's side she hears a tale of mental illness and more migration, as she follows in her ancestors' footsteps from Cornwall to the North East

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: Niall and Ava face major life changes as Ruben struggles with his relationship. An impulsive choice between Mona and Niall has serious consequences for all involved. Hard-hitting drama, written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell, Amy Manson and Anjli Mohindra

programme.title: The Photograph (2020)
programme.desc: Michael, a reporter, becomes fascinated by a picture of a woman, and wants to know her backstory. In the present day, following the death of her estranged mother, a devastated young woman finds a photo that will change her life forever. Writer/director Stella Meghie's drama, starring Issa Rae, LaKeith Stanfield and Chelsea Peretti

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Connor becomes increasingly anxious about his brother's disappearance and begs Pip to help him find Jamie. Despite her reluctance, she eventually realises she has no choice and is adamant Max must be involved somehow. But when she seemingly hits a dead end, she is forced to go back to the drawing board and retrace Jamie's last steps

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Highlights from series 19 of the comedy panel show hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. With appearances by Lucy Beaumont, Bob Mortimer, Jamelia, John Kearns, Harriet Kemsley, Yinka Bokinni, Gyles Brandreth, Dianne Buswell, Mark Chapman, Montell Douglas, Tasha Ghouri, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Will Kirk, David Morrissey, Josh Pugh and Holly Willoughby

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Hunting the Online Sex Predators
programme.desc: James Blake investigates whether social media is promoting harmful content, joining Cheshire Police's consent campaign against online risk and talking to Dr Kaitlyn Regehr, who explains how her research suggests one social media algorithm can quadruple misogynistic content in a week. He learns how it's easier to get a clip removed for copyrighted music than it is to get child abuse content taken down and realises the full extent of reports of harmful content on social media with the help of NCA officers

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy is hopeful that he could be fighting again soon, and he and Molly-Mae are spending more time together. Then, disaster strikes when KSI cancels the night when Roman was due to be in the fighting, claiming illness. John is furious, but decides the boys need cheering up so tells them he is taking them on a camping trip. However, neither Tommy nor Roman share their father's love of the outdoors

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy heads to Budapest to close the deal for the fight, but there's a hitch. With just four weeks to go, John calls the deal off because the money hasn't reached Tommy's account

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: It's Tommy's birthday, but there's no time for cake. With just days to go before his long-awaited comeback fight in Budapest, Tommy is pulled in every direction - juggling press conferences, promo shoots and the pressure of proving he's still got what it takes in the ring

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: After the success of his comeback fight, Tommy's road to redemption hits a major setback - his hand is injured again and needs eight weeks' total rest before doctors will know if he can fight again. The last time he couldn't train, his life spiralled out of control, so now Tommy throws himself into a frenzy of activity - a TV appearance on The Wheel, family days out with Bambi and a final push to finish the home he hopes Molly and Bambi will soon return to

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy's recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommy's next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mother out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair

programme.title: Minions: The Rise of Gru (2022)
programme.desc: Young Gru attends an interview to join a diabolical dream team christened the Vicious 6, but Belle Bottom, Jean-Clawed, Nun-chuck, Svengeance and Stronghold cruelly dismiss the boy. In retaliation for the ageist snub, the resourceful tyke steals an ancient amulet that can harness the power of the 12 creatures of the Chinese zodiac. Animated comedy prequel, featuring the voices of Steve Carell, Alan Arkin, Taraji P Henson and Jean-Claude Van Damme

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and her team help Cherelle and Tony Dowling transform their chaotic two-bedroom home through a radical space-saving reorganisation. With a lost engagement ring somewhere in the house and all their time taken up with tidying away the toys of their four children under four, the clutter is causing chaos. Carpenter Iwan Carrington gives the home a supersized spring clean, Robert Bent radically reconfigures the bedrooms to create maximum space, while Dilly Carter reveals the best way to sort out kids' rooms and kitchen cupboards

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: Blackmailed: The Sextortion Killers
programme.desc: Reporter Tir Dhondy investigates the rise of sextortion scams targeting teenage boys on social media, including the case of 16-year-old victim Evan Boettler. Her journey leads to Nigeria, where she confronts the scammers taking part in these crimes. With unprecedented access and interviews with industry insiders, Tir exposes a disturbing online underworld and its devastating consequences

programme.title: Who Stole Tamara Ecclestone's Diamonds?
programme.desc: The 2019 burglary of Tamara Ecclestone and Jay Rutland's London home. Evading a 24-hour security team on one of the capital's most secure streets, three men managed to gather more than 400 items of jewellery and watches valued at more than £26million. Racing against the clock to gather crucial CCTV material before it was erased, police uncovered a trail that led first to Italy then the Balkans. With access to the Ecclestones, the police investigation and even some of the suspects in the case, this documentary tells the full story of the biggest home burglary in British history

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: The case of an online catfishing abuse incident, which saw a 20-year-old suspect accused by a 12-year-old girl in Scotland. The suspect's bail conditions were supposed to prevent him from accessing the internet, but he very quickly was the subject of a second accusation from New Zealand, with online activity traced back to his house

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: Police re-arrest Alexander McCartney after discovering thousands of images of child abuse on his electronic devices and a multi-agency international investigation begins when the US Department of Homeland Security learn of two more catfishing victims. With McCartney due in court to plead guilty to charges of making indecent images of children and blackmail, the case takes a turn when an obituary page for one of his victims is found

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy's recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommy's next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mother out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy realises just how far he and Molly have come since the separation, but John's worries grow as Tommy takes on a 100km triathlon, pushing his body to new physical limits. Before the challenge, Tommy takes some downtime at the spa with his mates, preparing for a huge press push ahead of his series premiere. After completing a series of intense physical challenges, he has to deal with a social media backlash

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: Hunting the Rolex Rippers
programme.desc: The search for the gang of thieves known for stalking celebrities on social media and targeting those with expensive watches. Journalist Tir Dhondy gets access, for the first time ever, to the gang members behind the crime. She meets the teenage moped riders snatching watches off the street, as well as a gang leader who runs a team of teenage watch rippers and female watch spotters. Tir also comes face-to-face with the 16-year-old who used a machete to steal singer Aled Jones' £17,000 Rolex in broad daylight

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Single, penniless poet Ruby has recently moved back home to live with her mother, Jen. When their strict Presbyterian neighbours descend on the home for her spoiled sister Eliza's engagement party, Ruby's only job is to not shame the family. Short, one-off comedy, starring Amy Molloy

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve is in search of one of the most spectacular events on earth - bait balls. These wildlife events are so epic that they can be seen from space - but they won't be easy to find

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall searches for deadly animals living in the capital of Suriname, from snakes in people's gardens to snail-catching kites and one of the deadliest snakes on earth

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: After racing over 11,000km through Europe and Asia, the teams face one final test of endurance as they embark on the last leg of their journey to the finish line in Hatgal, Mongolia, where the first team to arrive will claim victory and a £20,000 prize

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Six months after returning home from the adventure of a lifetime, all five teams reunite for the very first time. Together, they look back on their extraordinary 12,000km journey from Palermo in Sicily to the remote wilderness of Hatgal in northern Mongolia, reliving the defining moments of a race that pushed them all to their limits. From the euphoric highs to the brutal setbacks, this celebration of their journey combines unseen footage with exclusive behind-the-scenes access

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some people's most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker revisits documentaries exploring the commodification of forbidden desires, taking in subjects ranging from the porn industry to the US opioid crisis. He is reunited with porn star JJ, who has turned his back on his old career to begin a new life in eastern Europe, and drug addict Katilia, who is still struggling to turn her life around

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Nida offers to get Mobeen a fake passport if he can steal something from the Turks. Will help from Uncle Shady and his big drill be enough to pull it off? Comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Tensions run high as the gang debates what to do with the stolen money. Jerry reveals his true colours, Emre investigates the theft, and Uncle Shady offers Mobeen a lifeline. Comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: On a plane alone, Mobeen recounts how it all started, with his teenage memories of the first job he did for Khan and the beginning of his brotherhood with Nate and Eight. Comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: Police connect the suicide of a 12-year-old girl in West Virginia to Alexander McCartney, opening a manslaughter investigation but in order to charge him they have to prove he foresaw the risk to her life. The case sets a new legal precedent in the UK and more than five years after the investigation began, McCartney is finally convicted and sentenced

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A man afflicted with chronic bad luck tries to reconnect with his dad after setting fire to the family home. Comedy short, starring Dylan Blore and Mark Bonnar

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Two emotionally unassembled young men start a play group, Funboys Fun Club, for childish young men to engage in some wholesome mucking about. When they come across severely depressed 60-year-old Marty, they try their best to bring some joy back to his life. Pilot for the sitcom, with Rian Lennon and Ryan Early

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some people's most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker revisits documentaries exploring the commodification of forbidden desires, taking in subjects ranging from the porn industry to the US opioid crisis. He is reunited with porn star JJ, who has turned his back on his old career to begin a new life in eastern Europe, and drug addict Katilia, who is still struggling to turn her life around

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: After racing over 11,000km through Europe and Asia, the teams face one final test of endurance as they embark on the last leg of their journey to the finish line in Hatgal, Mongolia, where the first team to arrive will claim victory and a £20,000 prize

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Tom Allen is the host for more stand-up showcases performed in front of a live audience at London's Hammersmith Apollo. Rosie Jones and Kae Kurd take to the stage, and other performers set to appear in this series include Sindhu Vee, Alex Edelman, Lou Sanders, Darren Harriott, Jessica Fostekew, Stephen Bailey, Desiree Burch, Paul McCaffrey, Jonny Pelham, Ardal O'Hanlon, Sara Barron, Mawaan Rizwan, Guz Khan, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Rhys James, Sarah Pascoe, Chris McCausland and Flo & Joan

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist and explorer ventures into more uncharted territory in pursuit of new discoveries and world firsts, beginning by heading to the Kamchatka Peninsula in the far east of Russia. He joins a team of world-class kayakers to take on the first descent of the Kronotsky River, setting out to paddle from source to sea, starting in the shadow of a still-active volcano and ending at the Pacific Ocean

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Matty and Leanne need to pull off one last roll of the dice as hostile forces close in and the evidence stacks up against them

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: TV presenter and keen gardener Katie Piper joins Diarmuid and Carol to visit three more gardens. The judges head to Colin's surprising rustic garden in suburban Ballymena, before seeing how Adrienne has transformed two acres near the windswept Glenshane Pass in the foothills of the Sperrin Mountains. Their final stop is in Dromore, Co Down, where Julietta has created a garden designed with her young family in mind, including a wild meadow playground and a courtyard filled with edible plants

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Roman Kemp meets a Glasgow children's entertainer who is desperate for a good night's sleep. Adding a hot bath to an evening routine has been shown to help improve sleep, reduce the risk of heart disease and even burn a few extra calories

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Kelly Cates is joined by Alan Shearer and Wayne Rooney for action from the final Premier League matches of the season, including Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park and Manchester City v Aston Villa at Etihad Stadium. The title race between Arsenal and City had been expected to go to the wire, with the Gunners looking to win the league for the first time since 2004, and the Citizens looking to claim the Premier for the seventh time under Pep Guardiola. Plus, Tottenham Hotspur v Everton, West Ham United v Leeds United, Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United, Burnley v Wolverhampton Wanderers, Fulham v Newcastle United, Liverpool v Brentford, Nottingham Forest v Bournemouth and Sunderland v Chelsea

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, who answer question on the specialist subjects of the life of Michael Palin's travel documentaries, Lady Gaga, Emily Dickinson, and BBC comedy Mum, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Louis's reunion with his family is underwhelming, while at Oakley, Daisy's plans to have the vineyard represented in the Behind the Vines showcase receive a mixed response from Gus and Tippy. Daisy approaches Hilary and Nic for information on the event, but is not entirely thrilled with what she learns. Tippy struggles with her confidence, while Gus's inability to master the art of the haggle leads Daisy to a negotiation with the attractive Ben - only to discover she may be flirting with the enemy. Drama, starring Rebecca Gibney and Charles Edwards

programme.title: Sicily: The Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Scott traces the history of Sicily, from the first arrival of ancient Greek settlers 3,000 years ago to the arrival of the Byzantine Empire. He begins his journey on the slopes of Mount Etna, Europe's largest active volcano, and discovers how the Greeks began to settle on Sicily's east coast - planting their olives and vines and building great city states that soon came to rival even Athens itself

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team value a diverse range of items in the Wiltshire garrison town of Tidworth, including a regimental silver centrepiece, rare French porcelain, valuable toys and an unusual sardine tin

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Muncaster, Cumbria, hundreds of people turn up with collectibles and treasures they want to take to auction. Antiques experts James Lewis and Caroline Hawley uncover a few gems, including a dazzling decanter and an art nouveau picture frame. Presenter Paul Martin also finds time to visit Coniston Water in the Lake District

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Cheryl Baker, Chris Bavin, Axel Blake and Sophie Willan test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets out on a trip across Belgium, starting his adventure by boarding the Eurostar at London's St Pancras International to travel to the Belgian capital Brussels. Once there, he visits the city's Theatre Royal, learning how an impassioned opera performance led to revolution on the streets and the birth of an independent nation

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts sets off across Hadrian's Wall to reveal the forgotten story of the Roman Army's secret weapon in Britain - their cavalry. The programme also follows team of archaeologists and historical re-enactors as they attempt to re-stage a Roman cavalry tournament for a public display in Carlisle

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan return with the wildlife show, with their new base being at the National Trust's Crom estate on the shores of Lough Erne in Co Fermanagh, with remote cameras rigged across the site to uncover wildlife stories unfolding in real time. Iolo Williams undertakes his own wildlife wanderings, broadcasting live from the Knepp Estate in Sussex, where wildlife is thriving and stand-out species are returning to the landscape.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Part two of two. The historian explores how the battle for American independence was not fought only on American soil, but also unfolded in Britain itself, with sabotage, espionage, and political unrest reshaping the empire. She reflects on a revolution that created an independent America and also reshaped Britain and its monarch

programme.title: Giorraíonn Beirt Bóthar
programme.desc: Tessa Fleming and Cormac Ó hÁdhmaill retrace the footsteps of legendary warrior Cú Chulainn, a journey taking them along the ancient route from Dundalk to Navan Fort, Armagh. They start out at Dundalk Bay, where they call in at Cú Chulainn's Castle, before setting out towards St Brigid's holy well at Faughart. Swapping their hiking boots for bicycles, they explore the Gap of the North, meet up with the Sunrise Social walking group and discover the enduring history of Killeavy Old Churches on the slopes of Slieve Gullion. In Irish

programme.title: Are You There God? It's Me, Margaret (2023)
programme.desc: When her family moves home, 11-year-old Margaret is uprooted from her life in New York City for the suburbs of New Jersey, going through the throes of puberty with new friends in a new school. She relies on her mother, who is also struggling to adjust to life outside the big city, and her adoring grandmother, who isn't happy they moved away.Family drama based on Judy Blume's bestseller, starring Abby Ryder Fortson, Rachel McAdams and Kathy Bates

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the Teifi, known as the queen of Welsh rivers. He heads upstream to the Teifi marshes and the old slate quarries near Cilgerran, where he finds a slow worm hunting in the open. He also meets Rod Bowen, a local coracleman, to learn about a 2,000-year-old fishing tradition. A major highlight occurs at Maes y Crugiau, where Iolo watches an otter as it hunts and eats an eel

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: When the bomb detonated inside Harvey's Wagon Wheel Casino, it left behind more than shattered concrete and twisted steel in the form of a maze of unanswered questions. However, a lucky break in the case led to a psychological stand-off where calculated risks unravelled, loyalties fractured, and the truth proved far more complicated than anyone imagined

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.sub-title: The Elon Musk Show: The Next Chapter
programme.desc: Picking up where the 2022 series left off, this film tracks Elon Musk's takeover of Twitter, the seismic fallouts of his personal life and his evolution into a political figure on both side of the Atlantic. With immersive archive footage and revealing testimony from childhood friends, political insiders and former employees

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ben Fogle presents an appeal on behalf of Hearing Dogs for Deaf People, a charity that provide support and trained assistance dogs to those living with deafness or hearing loss

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Family of four Melissa, Matt, Becky and Nick competes for cash by crossing the bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets out on a trip across Belgium, starting his adventure by boarding the Eurostar at London's St Pancras International to travel to the Belgian capital Brussels. Once there, he visits the city's Theatre Royal, learning how an impassioned opera performance led to revolution on the streets and the birth of an independent nation

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Banned Blood Sport and Spontaneous Combustion on a Farm
programme.desc: Farmers and police in the East Yorkshire town of Goole join forces to tackle a cruel countryside crime, as rural crime officer Sam Blakebrough leads the pursuit of men accused of setting dogs on hares for sport. In Caernarfon, Gwynedd, a dairy farmer's harvest goes up in flames when a devastating case of spontaneous combustion destroys thousands of pounds worth of animal feed. Over in Winsford, Cheshire, reporter Paul Colgan trains with a specialist fire-and-rescue team to learn how they save large farm animals in life-or-death situations

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The team dive deep to search for a precious tankard lost by a heartbroken son, and detectives Mui and Sam work together to try to solve the mystery of a drone found in Ullswater

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, who answer question on the specialist subjects of the life of the films of Judy Garland, the Cure, Hi-de-Hi! and the French wars of religion, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: As preparations for the showcase continue, Tippy, Gus and Daisy attempt to help put Louis back on track, following the break-up of his marriage. Nic and Vic look to a spiritual ritual to help with their dreams of a child. Daisy is overjoyed when Griffin visits, but warns him against initiating a casual relationship with a besotted Gus. Drama, starring Carrie Green and Cohen Holloway

programme.title: Sicily: The Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Michael Scott concludes his history of Sicily, telling the story of how the Christian Byzantine Empire was swept aside by a Muslim Arab invasion from North Africa. The island was transformed by new trade networks and agricultural techniques, and in the 19th century was unified with Italy - but the greatest threat to their prosperity then came from within and the rise of the Mafia

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team visits Warwick Castle where cigarette cards, ancient Peruvian figures and a celebrated furniture-maker's working drawings are among the finds. Presented by Michael Aspel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The experts assess items brought to the Blickling estate in Norfolk, and Paul Martin learns about Lord Lothian's key role in getting America involved in the Second World War

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Cheryl Baker, Chris Bavin, Axel Blake and Sophie Willan test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian rail journey continues in the Dutch speaking region of Flanders. In Antwerp, he discovers the city's historic multi-billion-pound diamond industry and drops in at the workshop of an expert. Heading to Belgium's third city Ghent, he visits the cathedral and marvels at a 15th-century Van Eyck masterpiece, the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus wants to impress the locals with a French version of the classic Sunday roast and find out how they enjoy their take on the sandwich

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan continue to celebrate the season at National Trust Crom in Northern Ireland. There is a look at a multitude of species in the waterways, revealing a hidden world playing out in the reed beds. Iolo Williams reveals some of the Knepp Estate's most secretive animals and meets the people passionate about rewilding this wildlife haven

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts an extended edition of the comedy panel quiz, with Alice Levine, Jason Manford and Rose Matafeo joining series regular Alan Davies to look at sugar and spice and all things nice

programme.title: If Walls Could Talk
programme.desc: The stage in Carrickmore's Patrician Hall has been host to many performances, from musicians like Sandie Shaw and Roy Orbison to Hollywood actors Stephen Rea and Liam Neeson. The annual Mid-Ulster Drama Festival continues to bring crowds from across Northern Ireland to the hall in Main Street

programme.title: Carl Frampton: On Probation
programme.desc: Carl is back with Probation Officer Toni, who finds out one of her Service Users who is on probation for serious assault is about to become a father again, and puts measures into place to safeguard the progress he has made. Meanwhile in Belfast, Carl meets a woman who successfully completed probation and has big plans for her future, but her struggles with alcohol continue to hang over her

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.sub-title: The Elon Musk Show: The Next Chapter
programme.desc: Picking up where the 2022 series left off, this film tracks Elon Musk's takeover of Twitter, the seismic fallouts of his personal life and his evolution into a political figure on both side of the Atlantic. With immersive archive footage and revealing testimony from childhood friends, political insiders and former employees

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon must remove part of his patient's tongue and reconstruct it to save her from an aggressive cancer, and surgeons perform a rare simultaneous pancreas and kidney transplant

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Discovered by Disaster
programme.desc: Unexpected things revealed by catastrophic disasters, featuring explorations of Seahenge's 4,000-year-old Bronze Age timber circle, an unusual Ukrainian canoe, ancient remains in a mudslide and a relic from Lake Mead's dry bed

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Delving into the mysteries surrounding the death of Elvis Presley in 1977, investigating the theories that he remained alive, secretly, for some time afterwards

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: People reveal details from their past lives as celebrities, while experts investigate evidence that suggests death may not be the end of one's existence

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: American couple and civil rights activists Betty and Barney Hill, who claimed they were abducted by extraterrestrials in a rural area of New Hampshire in September 1961. What really happened to the hills that night? Was this a real case of aliens among us or a couple just looking for attention?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Broadhaven is a sleepy community in south-west Wales, but since the late 1970s it has made headlines for being the UFO capital of Europe. In 1977, schoolchildren spotted a cigar-shaped aircraft. The sighting transforms the quiet seaside town into a UFO hot spot

programme.title: Discovered by Disaster
programme.desc: Documentary revealing mysterious curiosities that were once hidden from human eyes, only to be exposed by a calamitous incident. This episode focuses on the Mary Celestia wreck, discovers imperial relics from Iraq and examines a medieval man's remains and lost artefacts reshaping Native American history

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the history of Oxford during the Civil War, when the city became a power base for the royalist forces and a rival capital for nine years of conflict. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers why the city was such a good strategic position, while Alice takes up Charles I's favourite sport and learns to fire a 17th-century musket

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Canterbury to explore the Plantagenet era, when the powerful house held the throne between 1154 and 1485. She discovers the secrets of the city's cathedral, a centre of ecclesiastical power and also the site of the shocking murder of Thomas Becket. She also discovers the lasting impact of the Black Death and joins a modern-day pilgrimage in medieval garb. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson provides a bird's-eye view of how the religious institutions of the era still dominate the city's topography
