programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A rich widow falsely identifies one of her building's employees as the man who robbed her, and later she wants to help him, but it may be too late. With Bette Davis and James Congdon

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A dress shop owner is having serious financial problems, so one of her suppliers suggests hiring someone to break in and set her premises on fire so she can collect the insurance. Drama, starring Nancy Olson and Ralph Meeker

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the actress who has been nominated for eight Academy Awards, including for The Big Chill, The Natural, Fatal Attraction and Dangerous Liaisons

programme.title: Spymasters: The Great Spy Writers
programme.desc: The lives and secrets of spy authors in literature and film, featuring the work of Rudyard Kipling, John Buchan and Graham Greene, as well as clips from Michael Caine movie The Ipcress File - based on a book by Len Deighton

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A landlady appears kind and friendly - but her last two guests disappeared two years ago. Siobhan McKenna and Leonard Preston star

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A woman is cut down to size by her husband's loyal butler when it is discovered she is having an affair. Drama, starring John Gielgud, Joan Collins and Peter Bowles

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.sub-title: You'll Be the Death of Me
programme.desc: A woman becomes the target of her murderous spouse after she finds a button from one of his victims

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.sub-title: Grateful Dead - From Anthem to Beauty
programme.desc: The making of the Grateful Dead albums Anthem of the Sun and American Beauty

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of American actor and director, who starred in Jacob's Ladder, The Shawshank Redemption and The Player and won an Oscar for his role in Mystic River

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Elizabeth McGovern, Fiona Shaw and Kadeena Cox
programme.desc: Actresses Elizabeth McGovern and Fiona Shaw and Paralympic gold medallist Kadeena Cox are the celebrity sitters as a new group of portrait artists attempts to impress the judges

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A housewife befriends a stray cat and suddenly finds her life begins to change. Drama, starring Joseph Cotten and Wendy Hiller

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An American tourist on board a trans-Atlantic liner stakes everything on the biggest gamble of his life. Jack Weston and Gladys Spencer star

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A man decides to get rid of his lover permanently, but is in for a surprise when he gets home to find two detectives waiting for him. With Robert Horton and Fay Spain

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A lonely woman who waited years to marry the man she loves finds out a terrible secret about why he stalled for so long. With Barbara Bel Geddes and Don Dubbins

programme.title: Julie Andrews Forever
programme.desc: A look at the career of the Oscar-winning actress, who starred in The Sound of Music and Mary Poppins

programme.title: The Unstoppable Shirley MacLaine
programme.desc: A profile of the Oscar-winning American actress and author, celebrating a fearless career of reinvention, longevity and memorable performances

programme.title: Three Days of the Condor (1975)
programme.desc: A bookish CIA researcher returns to his field office to find all his colleagues have been murdered in his absence. When he tries to uncover the truth, he also ends up targeted for assassination by his own agency, and is forced to go on the run to discover the reasons why. Sydney Pollack's thriller, starring Robert Redford, Faye Dunaway, Cliff Robertson and Max von Sydow

programme.title: Queen: Greatest Video Hits 2
programme.desc: A collection of the band's promos from 1982 onwards, featuring the videos for Radio Ga Ga, I Want to Break Free, Under Pressure and more

programme.title: Catching Fire: The Story of Anita Pallenberg (2023)
programme.desc: Documentary about the Italian actress and artist, who was known as the muse of the Rolling Stones, based on an autobiographical manuscript discovered by her children after her death in 2017. With readings by Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Cold War & Cinema
programme.desc: A look at depictions of the era on film

programme.title: Succession
programme.desc: As the Waystar team discusses a pivotal recommendation to the board, Roman, Kendall and Shiv navigate a misstep with Matsson. Jeremy Strong, Sarah Snook and Kieran Culkin star

programme.title: Task
programme.desc: Tom and his team inch closer to breaking the case with help from an eyewitness, while Perry pays Maeve a visit and Robbie puts his plan in motion. Later, Tom follows a lead that takes him down a dangerous path

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: A robbery gone wrong leads to the death of Danver's bride-to-be while trying on a wedding dress. Once again, Pembleton and Bayliss disagree on theories of the crime

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Drama offering a look at the complexities of life in a social-media obsessed world, starring Zendaya, Jacob Elordi and Sydney Sweeney

programme.title: Heated Rivalry
programme.desc: After a night together, Ilya lets his guard down, sending Shane into a panic. Shane's relationship with a movie star intensifies the turmoil between them. Drama starring Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie about two ice-hockey players whose on-ice animosity conceals a secret romance between the two

programme.title: Sort Of
programme.desc: With the help of Sabi and friends, Aqsa and Izzy sort of take the next step. Paul and Bessy reconnect over a shared experience.

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming examines the hidden histories of cities, revealing the sights and sounds rarely witnessed by the public. He begins in London at St Paul's Cathedral, where he visits a place tourists never go, before exploring a hidden park and a little-known church. He also gets a guided tour of Soho from broadcaster Danny Baker

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Brixham residents raise money for a statue to commemorate fishermen lost at sea, but Pat Seddon's plan to support the cause by participating in a fundraising skydive worries her friends and relatives. Jan Hart goes trawling for scallops, and shares some ancient superstitions

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Pat Seddon considers selling up and visits a clairvoyant, while celebrity chef Mitch Tonks makes an appearance at Brixham's Crab Festival

programme.title: We Own This City
programme.desc: As Momodu Gondo finds himself in an interrogation, a detective traces a string of overdoses to Baltimore. Crime drama, starring Jon Bernthal

programme.title: We Own This City
programme.desc: Jenkins learns stark truths about policing as a new officer. Meanwhile, Rayam opens up about the GTTF, and Suiter is assigned to a new case. Crime drama, starring Jon Bernthal

programme.title: We Own This City
programme.desc: Despite numerous complaints, Hersl is placed on the GTTF. Jensen monitors Gondo's calls, and Jenkins brings Suiter along on a raid. Crime drama, starring Jon Bernthal

programme.title: We Own This City
programme.desc: Maurice Ward recounts how Jenkins remained unscathed despite a series of illegal incidents. Baltimore is rocked by the Freddie Gray protests. Crime drama, starring Jon Bernthal

programme.title: We Own This City
programme.desc: Jensen, Sieracki and Leo Wise prepare to charge the officers of the GTTF. Suiter's past closes in on him, and Steele and Davis seek support

programme.title: We Own This City
programme.desc: Suiter worries over his subpoena, Jenkins learns his fellow officers are cooperating with the investigation, and Davis and the mayor's office clash

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Prison transport officer Amber's first day back after maternity sees her escorting ruthless contract killer Tibor Stone to court so he can testify in a high-profile case. When their convoy is attacked by a highly organised unit, Amber handcuffs herself to the prisoner to prevent his escape, but Tibor is a marked man and their assailants will stop at nothing to make sure he does not make it to court. Action thriller, starring Tahar Rahim and Izuka Hoyle

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Held captive at a secret weapons factory, Amber and Tibor narrowly escape execution by Nina before agreeing to a deal to help each other. Tibor leads Amber's husband Olly to a safe house, saving her family from Pegasus

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: While being tracked down, Amber and Tibor turn the tables on Nina and hitch a ride to London in the back of a lorry, unaware that their driver has sold them out. Action thriller, starring Tahar Rahim and Izuka Hoyle

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Amber reunites with her family while Tibor faces his estranged mother, while Olly rejects their plan and contacts the National Crime Unit, leading Pegasus straight to them

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Separated and under pressure, Amber turns to Tibor for help as a wider threat begins to emerge. Action thriller, starring Tahar Rahim and Izuka Hoyle

programme.title: Fish Town

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: August arrives and the fishermen of Brixham spend time ashore with their families as their boats are repaired - but summer proves costly for Jan, who loses £1,000 for every day his vessel is out of action. programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Following the opening of the bank, Alma charms Merrick into endorsing her liquidity, and it isn't long before she reverts to her old ways. Swearengen tries to figure out Hearst's intentions as he finally finds a use for Farnum. Meanwhile, two more actors arrive in town to be reunited with Langrishe as the troupe prepares to transform Chez Ami into a theatre. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Bullock and Swearengen contemplate a pre-emptive strike against Hearst, whose anger has been inflamed of late. programme.title: Hotel Mumbai (2018)
programme.desc: In 2008, at the Taj Mahal Palace Hotel, four terrorists take the staff and residents hostage. However, some of the employees risk their lives to protect their guests. Writer/director Anthony Maras's fact-based thriller, starring Dev Patel, Armie Hammer and Jason Isaacs

programme.title: The Italian Job (2003)
programme.desc: A crook double-crosses his partners in crime, a betrayal that leaves their friend and mentor dead. One year later, they regroup and come up with a daring plan to exact revenge on the traitor and reclaim their ill-gotten gains. Crime thriller inspired by the 1969 movie, starring Mark Wahlberg, Charlize Theron, Edward Norton, Seth Green and Donald Sutherland

programme.desc: Dive into a world of unmissable Sky Original films, from psychological thriller To Catch a Killer to the Oscar-nominated The Unites States vs Billie Holiday

programme.title: Red Dragon (2002)
programme.desc: The FBI agent who first captured Hannibal Lecter is called out of retirement to catch a demented murderer dubbed the Tooth Fairy, who slaughters a family whenever there is a full moon - forcing the detective to draw on the imprisoned cannibal's expertise in criminal psychology. Thriller prequel adapted from the first of Thomas Harris's Lecter novels, starring Anthony Hopkins, Edward Norton, Ralph Fiennes and Philip Seymour Hoffman

programme.title: Detroit (2017)
programme.desc: A group of rogue police officers responds to a complaint with apparent retribution rather than justice in mind, launching a raid on a group of African-Americans. Public anger reaches a peak after the incident, as relationships sour with authority. Fact-based drama set during the 1967 Detroit riots, starring John Boyega, Anthony Mackie and Algee Smith

programme.title: Eye in the Sky (2015)
programme.desc: Colonel Katherine Powell's mission to wipe out a terrorist cell in Kenya is complicated when a girl enters the blast radius of the intended drone strike. Powell has to decide between sacrificing the girl's life or letting the terrorists flee to carry out more atrocities. Thriller, starring Helen Mirren, Alan Rickman and Aaron Paul

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows (2011)
programme.desc: The detective investigates a series of seemingly unconnected crimes all around the world, and becomes convinced one man is behind them all - the elusive Professor Moriarty. He sets forth on an international journey to bring the evil mastermind to justice, but finds he is dealing with a foe who is his intellectual equal. Action thriller sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Jude Law, Noomi Rapace and Jared Harris

programme.title: Nightcrawler (2014)
programme.desc: A cynical petty crook is inspired when he sees a freelance camera crew, and decides to go into business filming crime scenes and accidents, then selling the footage to news broadcasters. His determination to stay ahead of the competition leads him down a dark path as his methods become increasingly ruthless. Crime drama, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Rene Russo and Bill Paxton

programme.title: Detroit (2017)
programme.desc: A group of rogue police officers responds to a complaint with apparent retribution rather than justice in mind, launching a raid on a group of African-Americans. Public anger reaches a peak after the incident, as relationships sour with authority. Fact-based drama set during the 1967 Detroit riots, starring John Boyega, Anthony Mackie and Algee Smith

programme.title: Ocean's 8 (2018)
programme.desc: Following in the footsteps of brother Danny, light-fingered Debbie Ocean plans to steal the Toussaint, a $150million necklace from Vogue's Met Gala, with the 'help' of a famous actress. Crime caper, starring Sandra Bullock, Cate Blanchett, Anne Hathaway and Mindy Kaling

programme.title: Captain Phillips (2013)
programme.desc: An American cargo ship sets a dangerous course around the coast of Somalia, while inland, four men are pressed into service as pirates by the local warlords. The captain is taken hostage when the raiding party hijacks the vessel, resulting in a tense five-day crisis. Fact-based thriller, starring Tom Hanks and Barkhad Abdi

programme.title: The Bourne Legacy (2012)
programme.desc: The leaders of a secret government agency plan to eliminate several agents as part of a cover-up when they face having their dark secrets exposed. One of the targets is able to evade his fate and go on the run - but his escape is threatened by his dependence on drugs to boost his physical and mental capabilities. Spy thriller sequel, with Jeremy Renner, Rachel Weisz and Edward Norton

programme.title: The Mechanic (2011)
programme.desc: A professional hitman lets his emotional detachment slip when his mentor is murdered, and puts his deadly skills to the task of getting revenge. The solitary assassin finds an ally in his teacher's son, who also yearns for vengeance and persuades the killer to teach him the tricks of the trade. Action thriller remake, starring Jason Statham, Ben Foster and Donald Sutherland.

programme.title: Serenity (2019)
programme.desc: Baker Dill lives a peaceful life as a fishing boat captain, leading tours off a tranquil cove. His peace is shattered when estranged ex-wife Karen reappears, begging for him to help save her and their son from her violent husband. Thriller, written and directed by Peaky Blinders creator Steven Knight, starring Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway and Diane Lane

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview - May 2026
programme.desc: A look ahead at the films to enjoy this month on Sky Cinema

programme.title: Eye in the Sky (2015)
programme.desc: Colonel Katherine Powell's mission to wipe out a terrorist cell in Kenya is complicated when a girl enters the blast radius of the intended drone strike. programme.title: The Bourne Legacy (2012)
programme.desc: The leaders of a secret government agency plan to eliminate several agents as part of a cover-up when they face having their dark secrets exposed. One of the targets is able to evade his fate and go on the run - but his escape is threatened by his dependence on drugs to boost his physical and mental capabilities. Spy thriller sequel, with Jeremy Renner, Rachel Weisz and Edward Norton

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. An industrialist hires him to perform a far more challenging job - to implant an idea into a corporate heir's mind, so he will think it is his own. However, the mission is compromised by the thief's own troubled psyche. Christopher Nolan's sci-fi thriller, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Elliot Page, Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Tom Hardy

programme.title: Jason Bourne (2016)
programme.desc: Former government operative Jason Bourne has been in hiding for 12 years after exposing a sinister CIA operation. However, he once again finds himself being hunted down when a former CIA operative gone rogue acquires top secret information - including details of Bourne's own father's involvement in a clandestine scheme. Action thriller, starring Matt Damon, Tommy Lee Jones, Alicia Vikander and Julia Stiles

programme.title: The Meg (2018)
programme.desc: A massive creature attacks a deep-sea submersible, leaving the crew of the disabled vessel trapped at the bottom of the Pacific. Rescue diver Jonas Taylor is hired to save the crew from a 75ft-long prehistoric shark, known as the Megalodon. Director Jon Turteltaub's action thriller, starring Jason Statham, Bingbing Li, Rainn Wilson and Ruby Rose

programme.title: 300: Rise of an Empire (2014)
programme.desc: Ruler of the Persian empire and self-proclaimed god Xerxes continues his campaign to crush the emerging Greek nation, forcing a general from the democratic city state of Athens to lead his navy into battle against Xerxes' vast armada. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Sullivan Stapleton, Eva Green and Lena Headey

programme.title: The Hurricane Heist (2018)
programme.desc: Thieves attempt a major heist against the US Treasury as a Category 5 hurricane approaches one of its mint facilities. Action thriller, starring Toby Kebbell, Maggie Grace and Ryan Kwanten.

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: Don't Let's Go to the Dogs Tonight (2025)
programme.desc: An eight-year-old girl experiences life on her family's Rhodesian farm during the final stages of the Bush War. Drama, written, directed by and starring Embeth Davidtz, with Lexi Ventner and Zikhona Bali

programme.title: Tafiti - Across The Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie (2025)
programme.desc: When Gabby's prized dollhouse ends up with eccentric cat lady Vera, the youngster embarks on a adventure to reunite the Gabby Cats and retrieve her beloved diorama before it's too late. Comedy adventure, starring Laila Lockhart Kraner and Kristen Wiig

programme.title: The Running Man (2025)
programme.desc: Desperate to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards enters a deadly televised competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins. Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: Wuthering Heights (2026)
programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw 'acquires' an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennell's drama based on Emily Bronte's romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: The Running Man (2025)
programme.desc: Desperate to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards enters a deadly televised competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins. Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: One Last Deal (2026)
programme.desc: A veteran sports agent scrambles to land one last high-stakes deal for his star client, with both their futures on the line. Drama, starring Danny Dyer and Elliott Rogers

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: We Bury the Dead (2024)
programme.desc: When an experimental military weapon accidentally devastates Tasmania, volunteers travel to the island to help authorities identify the dead. Among them is a Ava Newman, who secretly hopes to find her missing husband. However, some of the bodies seem to be returning to life. Horror, starring Daisy Ridley, Brenton Thwaites and Mark Coles Smith

programme.title: The Cinema List: Race Against Time
programme.desc: This edition is about the race against time, from a deadly threat in Fuze to The Desperate Hour, where Naomi Watts faces every parent's nightmare - the clock is ticking

programme.title: The Real CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Rapper XXXTentacion's murder at 20-years-old shook hip-hop. Investigators used forensics to uncover motives behind killing

programme.title: Murder Uncut
programme.desc: Gretchen Anthony's family grow suspicious when they receive odd texts from her phone, before things take an incredibly sinister turn

programme.title: Murdered at First Sight
programme.desc: As 13-year-old Lauren Landavoza walks home from school, a 22-year-old loner is getting ready to ensure that if he can't have her, no one can

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of child killer Roy Whiting, the convicted sex offender who abducted and murdered eight-year-old Sarah Payne

programme.title: The Real Manhunter
programme.desc: After being picked up by NHS worker Duncan Bone at King's Cross in 1992, Karl Warne and Colin Lockett would murder their host at his flat

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shop assistant is terrified when armed robbers burst in, while police try to catch a carer who steals money from elderly victims

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood returns with the programmes that reveals how the police and the public are using CCTV evidence and technology to catch criminals in the act. A specialist crime squad stop a mugger on a moped in his tracks, intruders prowl the tiny streets of a model village in the dead of night, and insurance cheats are brought to justice when their fake falls are exposed

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A woman is dragged to the ground in a doorstep robbery, launderette owners use wanted posters to catch thieves, and criminals drive their own cars into the hands of the police

programme.title: Brit Cops
programme.desc: Documentary series which follows a rapid response team of police officers as they tackle crime

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reports on how a detective's extraordinary ability to read body language brought a pair of international crooks to justice. Plus, the burglar who tried to batter his way into a chip shop and how inebriated night clubbers got a big shock when they took a short cut home

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A trap is set to catch a pair of bogus builders, a thief regrets leaving his own bike behind at the scene of his crime, and a burglar is too drunk to make his escape

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Detectives trail a burglar who is amongst the most callous they have ever encountered, and a trusted employee runs out of luck when she is caught stealing scratch cards

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Two cars are stolen from a family's driveway, and fly-tippers leave clues among the rubbish dumped at a beauty spot. Presented by Dom Littlewood

programme.title: Caught on Dashcam
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-dashboard documentary showcasing outrageous road disasters, captured in video form by drivers' in-vehicle dashboard cameras

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: The story of 22-year-old Emma Baum, who was savagely battered to death by her ex-boyfriend David Davies in a row over access to their child

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: The 2019 murder of Raymond Cullen that sparked an investigation where forensic and digital evidence exposed a shocking, calculated plot behind his death

programme.title: Murder Next Door
programme.sub-title: The Murder of Peter and Sylvia Stuart
programme.desc: When a body is found in woodlands hours after an elderly couple are reported missing, the residents in the village are shocked there's a killer in their midst

programme.title: The Suzy Lamplugh Mystery
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring one of the most baffling cases in recent history - the mysterious disappearance of estate agent Suzy Lamplugh from Fulham, south west London. The last clue to her whereabouts was an appointment in July 1986 to show a house to someone she referred to as 'Mr Kipper'

programme.title: The Suzy Lamplugh Mystery
programme.desc: Part two of two. Documentary exploring one of the most baffling cases in recent history - the mysterious 1986 disappearance of the London estate agent. With no body, Suzi was officially declared dead, presumed murdered, in 1994

programme.title: The Murderer & Me
programme.desc: Factual crime stories. Over a 10-day period in 2013, Joanna Dennehy murdered three men in and around Peterborough, and then fled to Hereford, where she went on to stab two dog walkers in an apparent indiscriminate murder attempt. This documentary tells the story of the Home Counties mother of two who became one of Britain's most notorious killers

programme.title: The Murderer & Me
programme.desc: The first wife of serial killer Peter Tobin reveals her terrifying first-hand account of his violence, adding a new layer to the story of his gruesome murders

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look at John Cooper, a Welsh serial killer known as 'the Wildman', who got four life sentences for a string of gruesome murders and sexual assaults

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: Thirty years after the gruesome murders of three women, familial DNA and the exhumation of a body finally closes the case

programme.title: The Bambers: Murder at the Farm
programme.desc: The true story of the White House Farm murders, where Jeremy Bamber took the lives of his adoptive parents, sister and her twin sons in 1985

programme.title: The Bambers: Murder at the Farm
programme.desc: Jeremy's mammoth trial divides the public. He alleges his sister committed the murders, but a crucial piece of evidence contradicts his story

programme.title: The Bambers: Murder at the Farm
programme.desc: Bamber is imprisoned for life, but decides to challenge the verdict and clear his name. A band of campaigners and internet sleuths support him

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of David Berkowitz, the serial killer who found himself at the centre of New York's biggest manhunt after a vicious shooting spree that left six dead

programme.title: Ireland's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Delving into Ireland's worst murder cases. In 2004, Joe O'Reilly murdered his wife and made it look like a burglary gone wrong. Before being caught, he even appealed for information on TV

programme.title: Murders That Shocked the Nation
programme.sub-title: Harold Jones - The Welsh Child Killer
programme.desc: The case of Harold Jones, found guilty of murder at the age of 15 in 1921, who is also suspected to have been 1960s serial killer 'Jack the Stripper'

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A case highlighting the dangers of online grooming, in which 14-year-old Breck Bednar was lured to a London flat before being killed by a fellow teenager

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: PC Greaves and his four-year-old German shepherd Riley track down a motorist who panicked when he saw flashing blue lights and ran off, while PC Whitaker and his dog Roscoe have to disarm a man who has a 12-inch knife. During a search of three vehicles used by a suspected drug dealer, sniffer spaniel Smut and handler PC Carlin find a wallet with almost £1,000 inside it hidden under a dashboard. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness the best and worst behaviour on the nation's busiest motorway

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness the best and worst behaviour on the nation's busiest motorway

programme.title: Brit Cops
programme.desc: Documentary series which follows a rapid response team of police officers as they tackle crime

programme.title: Highway Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of New Zealand police officers as they try to keep motorists safe on some of the country's most dangerous roads

programme.title: Highway Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of New Zealand police officers as they try to keep motorists safe on some of the country's most dangerous roads

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: It's journey's end for a pair of jokers who think it's a jape to take a bus for a joyride

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reveals how a gang of international bag thieves were brought down to earth by an aviation crime squad. Plus, thefts from an elderly woman - were her carers or her kin to blame?

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shopkeeper tears the balaclava from his attacker, items mysteriously vanish from a locked house, and a villager reunites some rubbish with its rightful owner

programme.title: Caught on Dashcam
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-dashboard documentary showcasing outrageous road disasters, captured in video form by drivers' in-vehicle dashboard cameras

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A fugitive from the United States, David Bieber, murders one British police officer and tries to kill two more, sparking a nationwide search

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: The story of a young wife and mother-of-four, murdered ferociously by her controlling husband and an accomplice

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: When the body of a 16-year-old girl is found in a field, it would take over two decades before a DNA profile of the killer can be developed

programme.title: Death on the Beach
programme.desc: In 2015 Chrissie Annesley travelled to the paradise island of Koh Tao for the trip of a lifetime. Two weeks later she was found dead in her beach hut, the latest in a tragic roll call of young travellers to have died under mysterious circumstances on this most remote of Thailand's islands. This documentary asks whether Chrissie and the others were victims of misadventure or part of a series of violent murders

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: How police used CCTV, eyewitness statements and a public appeal to find the killer of a 28-year-old woman, found dead on Nottingham's streets

programme.title: Murdered at First Sight
programme.desc: The tragic story of Debbie Buxton, who was walking her dogs at a local beauty spot when she was killed in a frenzied and unprovoked attack by David Bond

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: The horrific case of Georgina Edmonds, a pensioner and grandmother who was bludgeoned to death by Matthew Hamlen

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: For Richard Kuklinski, crime was his way of life. As a professional murderer, his unusual methods led to a disturbing nickname, The Iceman

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look at Vincent Tabak, the man responsible for the murder of Joanna Yeates, which triggered one of the biggest-ever police investigations in Bristol

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: Experts analyse sand found in a car after a man goes missing, leading them to a body and a twisted murder case

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: A look at the murder of 15-year-old Holly Newton, who was stabbed to death in an alleyway in the quiet town of Hexham

programme.title: The Bambers: Murder at the Farm
programme.desc: Jeremy's relationship with his parents comes under the microscope, revealing possible motives for the murders. The tabloids hunt his girlfriend, Julie

programme.title: The Real Manhunter
programme.desc: The story of Michael Danaher, who found himself deep in debt, prompting him to draw up a list of famous and wealthy robbery targets. But his plans resulted in vicious murder

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The lives and crimes of two cousins who, together, took the lives of 10 girls and women aged between 12 and 28

programme.title: Ireland's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: When Mary Gough was found dead in 2001, her husband appeared shocked before his web of lies and deeply sinister intentions were brought to light

programme.title: Murders That Shocked the Nation
programme.sub-title: Derek Bentley - Let Him Have It!
programme.desc: The case of Derek Bentley, who was hanged for the murder of a policeman during a burglary in Wandsworth, south London, in 1952, even though it was his 16-year-old accomplice Christopher Craig who pulled the trigger

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: After a teenager's disappearance, a huge search campaign results in a truly depraved case striking at the very heart of a family

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.desc: Chevy Chase was a cast member on the inaugural series of Saturday Night Live and he built on that success to star in numerous Hollywood comedies and host the Academy Awards. He played hapless father Clark Griswold in the National Lampoon films and ran amok in pictures including Caddyshack, Spies Like Us, Fletch and Three Amigos. Two-time Emmy Award winner Marina Zenovich directs an authorised feature-length documentary portrait of Chase, which includes rare and intimate home movies and archive footage. Chase and his family share memories directly to camera, enriched with contributions from friends and collaborators including Dan Aykroyd, Beverly D'Angelo, Goldie Hawn, Lorne Michaels, Ryan Reynolds and Martin Short.

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: In January 2008, a shadow was cast over the town of Stephenville, Texas, when a UFO passed overhead at supersonic speed. The event was witnessed by scores of stunned locals who remain desperate for answers on what exactly they saw that winter's evening. Craig and space journalist Sarah Cruddas talk to eyewitness private pilot Steve Allen who was with friends when he saw a vast UFO passing overhead at a speed estimated to be 2,000mph. He describes the craft as having an array of bright, high-intensity lights at its rear - and pursued by two US Air Force F16 fighter jets. The team discovers that the US military, who initially denied any knowledge of this event, changed their story in the weeks following the incident, adding fuel to the theory that there could be a high-level cover-up

By September 6 the city's anti-aircraft defences had been reduced to a few guns and much of the Polish capital was in ruins. The bomber aircraft dominated the skies throughout the rest of the Second World War as fleets stood ready on the airfields of every combatant nation

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts travels to Bamburgh, Northumberland, to learn about the generations of Anglo Saxons who lived and worked there, gaining an insight into the illnesses they suffered and the societies in which they lived. She visits the Sutton Hoo site, as well as a pioneering project in Sittingbourne, Kent, which was set up following the unearthing of an Anglo Saxon cemetery, and looks at the Staffordshire hoard discovered by a metal detector in 2009

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts finds out about discoveries that shed light on the Tudor Age, visiting excavations at Shakespeare's first theatre in London's Shoreditch and at his last home in Stratford-upon-Avon, where clues reveal his economical use of money. In Wales, she meets a team of archaeologists learning about the realities of Henry VIII's dissolution of the monasteries, while the history of a forgotten royal palace is uncovered on the banks of the Thames

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams visit archaeological digs across the country, beginning with a look at the work being carried out in the east of England. The presenters visit Norwich Castle Museum, where they find out about developments at Must Farm in Cambridgeshire, a hunt for Roman treasure in Colchester, a burial site at Abington, an Anglo-Saxon royal hall at Lyminge, the 16th-century Tudor Basing House, and a dig involving Emperor Nero at Silchester

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams visit the Dorset Country Museum, where they explore finds from archaeological digs across the west of Britain. The presenters examine objects found at a Bronze Age burial site in Barrowclump, a Roman army site called Durotriges and the site of an early hospital at Winchester, Hampshire. Plus, a hoard found by metal detectors revealing hidden secrets of childhood 1,000 years ago, and a Roman settlement at Ipplepen in Devon

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: North - Viking Boat and Tudor Feasts
programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams explore finds from archaeological digs across the north of Britain, and begin at the Ness of Brodgar in the Heart of Neolithic Orkney World Heritage Site, where they view a 5,000-year-old temple. The presenters witness the unearthing of a Roman altar in Maryport, Cumbria, examine discoveries from Flixton in North Yorkshire that are around 11,000 years old, and travel to a cemetery in Ardnamurchan in the Highlands. Plus, a Tudor-era feasting hall, and a hoard of Pictish treasures

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Military Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Ghost Armies, Missing Pilot, Lost Bomber, Inside the AC-130
programme.desc: Combat veterans analyse controversial wartime images, documents and recordings spanning global wars and modern conflicts, aided by experts to uncover mysteries and alleged cover-ups. This programme investigates the fate of a Desert Storm fighter pilot who was being shot down in Iraq and the survival of six Second World War pilots after their B-24 bomber crashed in the Libyan Desert

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: A look at some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, as captured soldiers, sailors and airmen on both sides dared the impossible to win their freedom. They dug tunnels, stole planes, forced their way through deadly jungles, and marched huge distances pursued by death squads. This episodes focuses on Colditz Castle, the Great Escape and the bold Warburg Wire Job

programme.desc: The January 2008 mass UFO sightings between Fort Worth, Texas and Houston, with focus on the nearby town of Stephenville, Texas

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: In North Yorkshire, Beau Ouimette, Rick Edwards and Gary Bankhead search both the River Nidd and River Swale for lost weaponry from the brutal battles in 1318 and 1319 between the English and the Scottish. Among a number of finds, the trio uncover a lead token dating back to the 1300s and the time of the Scottish raiders

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.sub-title: Invincible - The Royal Navy's Greatest Ship
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands examine the history of HMS Invincible, which was captured from the French in 1747 and helped transform the Royal Navy with the lessons it taught about organisation and ship design. For more than 260 years, HMS Invincible lay at the bottom of the Solent, but archaeologists are now diving beneath the waves in a £2m excavation to discover more about this game-changing ship

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: Bizarre ice formations in the Antarctic Ocean are investigated and a discovery off the east coast of Canada may be connected to a tragic event. In the most remote, cold, and inhospitable places on the planet, scientists and adventurers have uncovered mysteries that have been frozen in time

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: The skilled 'pickers' strike gold in Quebec, where they find an unusual Inuit carving and heaps of antique farming equipment. Plus, Scott puts in an outrageous offer for a rare tin can

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In a feature-length episode, theorist Giorgio Tsoukalos explores the latest scientific discoveries at some of the world's most ancient places to find evidence that extraterrestrials visited Egypt in the distant past

programme.title: History of Weapons
programme.desc: Military historian Stephen Bull tests the three most significant weapons of the sea and explores whether the subsonic torpedo could rule the waves in the future

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: A look at some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, featuring a German breakout, a US bomber crew's Soviet secret and the Trojan Horse of Stalag Luft III

programme.title: Bomber: Terror of WWII
programme.desc: The story of a deadly war that intertwines Germany, Poland, Japan, Italy and Spain as the remorseless development of weapons and bombers

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In a feature-length episode, theorist Giorgio Tsoukalos explores the latest scientific discoveries at some of the world's most ancient places to find evidence that extraterrestrials visited Egypt in the distant past

programme.title: Bomber: Terror of WWII
programme.desc: The story of Adolf Hitler's so-called 'miracle' weapons - the V1 cruise missile and the V2 ballistic missile. These long-range artillery weapons posed a significant threat to the Allies

programme.title: WWII Battles in Colour
programme.desc: The most important battles of the European theatre. Rare archive, colourised for the first time, charts the German Army in 1939 as it unleashes a new Blitzkrieg - lightning war - tactic. This documentary features radio broadcasts, oral histories, memoirs and personal diaries from both sides of the battle lines. Supporting the words of the combatants is expert analyses by military and social historians

programme.title: WWII Battles in Colour
programme.desc: Gripping real life stories - when the fate of nations hung in the balance - are brought to life in never-before-seen colourised battle footage. This instalment illustrates life and death on the battlefield of El Alamein and life behind the lines in the north African desert

programme.title: WWII Battles in Colour
programme.desc: The Battle of Kursk, a Second World War engagement between German and Soviet forces on the Eastern Front near Kursk, roughly 280 miles south west of Moscow, during July and August 1943. This archive footage, colourised for the first time, tells the story of the greatest tank battle the world has ever seen

programme.title: WWII Battles in Colour
programme.desc: The Battle of the Bulge, a major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, which took place in December 1944 and January 1945. Film, colorised for the first time, brings eye-witness accounts from those who fought in the US military's bloodiest battle on the European front in the conflict

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Military Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Jungle Horrors, Project Giant, Homesick Mechanic, Inside Narco Subs
programme.desc: Efforts by the US military during the Vietnam War to convince the enemy it was under attack by evil spirits. Plus, the theft of one of the most powerful military planes in the world by a man who had never flown a plane and the facts behind a giant underground base built by the Nazis during the Second World War

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: Some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, including a daring dash through Nazi Europe, a deadly mountain escape and the Second World War's largest breakout, by men who chose death over dishonour

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: Some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, including a daring dash through Nazi Europe, a deadly mountain escape and the Second World War's largest breakout, by men who chose death over dishonour

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Veteran actor William Shatner examines the possibility of contact with otherworldly intelligent life, and how man should react to and handle such a situation. Plus, the practicality of man travelling beyond the moon

programme.title: Secrets in the Dark
programme.desc: Documentary shedding light on unusual discoveries made in the darkest places on Earth and venturing into the realm of the supernatural, where eerie and unsettling findings turn up unexplained phenomena that challenge man's understanding of the world. This episode visits the ancient Burnt City of Shahre Sukhteh, Iran, as archaeologists uncover a remarkable discovery - the oldest-known prosthetic eye, made of gold

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In a feature-length episode, theorist Giorgio Tsoukalos explores the latest scientific discoveries at some of the world's most ancient places to find evidence that extraterrestrials visited Egypt in the distant past

programme.title: UFO Hunters
programme.desc: The team examines a declassified US Department of Defence report which analyses an alleged encounter between a fighter pilot and a UFO in May 1980

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: An investigation into the last pitched battle to have taken place on British soil, the Battle of Culloden in 1746, on the outskirts of Inverness. Rick Edwards and river detectorist Beau Ouimette search for clues as to where Bonnie Prince Charlie, crossed the River Nairn as he fled the battlefield pursued by government forces. The battle was to have an impact for decades to follow on those on the losing side, the Jacobites who wanted to restore the Stuart lineage to the British throne. The Jacobite forces were shot down as they carried out their famous Highland Charge across the moorland, and some of those who survived escaped and crossed the River Nairn in an attempt to return to the relative safety of their homelands

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Archaeologists conduct a dig at Auckland Castle in Co Durham, which 700 years ago was home to a group of power-crazed bishops who behaved more like warriors than religious leaders. The experts head to Dirleton Castle in East Lothian to reveal the role one of the men played in defending England from William Wallace's army, and also try their hands at the art of stained glass-making, and fire a working trebuchet - the ultimate medieval weapon of war

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: Mysterious industrial waste and abandoned homes are discovered in a remote, uninhabited part of Greenland. In Norway, strange ditches and huge mounds are found deep within the Arctic Circle. A huge pyramid rivalling those of ancient Egypt is found far out on the Kazakh steppe. Plus, a perfectly shaped ball of golden fur melts out of the frozen ground of the Yukon's gold fields

programme.title: Bradley Walsh: Egypt's Cosmic Code
programme.desc: The presenter and one-time Rolls Royce engineer fulfils a lifetime's ambition to visit the monuments of Ancient Egypt in a bid to understand how it could have been possible to produce such 'wonders of the ancient world' 4,500 years ago. An expert guide is on hand to lay out the mainstream thinking and act as the perfect 'foil' for Bradley and his theories

programme.title: Bradley Walsh: Egypt's Cosmic Code
programme.desc: The presenter dives deeper into Egypt's mysteries, exploring the Great Sphinx and the underground Serapeum with its precision-cut granite sarcophagi, questioning their purpose and links to the pyramid enigma. Travelling south, he visits the intricately designed Dendera Temple, where hieroglyphs and imagery hint at possible extraterrestrial connections. He concludes at the sprawling Karnak temple complex, marvelling at its monumental engineering, sphinx-lined avenues, and Hatshepsut's towering obelisk - wondering whether it's simply a monument or a channel for cosmic energy

programme.title: Bradley Walsh: Egypt's Cosmic Code
programme.desc: The presenter sets out to uncover the mysteries of King Tutankhamun. Along the way, he encounters the enigmatic 'Alien King' Akhenaten and his wife, Nefertiti. Sailing down the Nile, he investigates how the Ancient Egyptians transported enormous stone blocks for their architectural marvels, including the 700-ton Colossi of Memnon. His quest then leads him into the Valley of the Kings, where he discovers the resting places of Akhenaten and King Tut's successor, Horemheb - before finally coming face to face with the mummy of the Boy King himself

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Elephant
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough investigates the life of Jumbo, a famous Victorian elephant, supposedly the largest that ever lived. Jumbo arrived at London Zoo in 1865 and was popular with the royal family, but was dangerously unhappy in captivity. In later life, he would be sold to PT Barnum's circus, becoming a celebrity before dying under mysterious circumstances. Attenborough examines Jumbo's remains and considers the lives of elephants in the wild to learn more about the animal's difficult life

programme.title: Wild Castles
programme.desc: Explore the historic home of an extraordinary family of birds of prey in a look at the wildlife living in the nature reserve surrounding Germany's Heidelberg Castle

programme.title: Impossible Animals
programme.desc: Patrick Aryee examines the animals that live on the coast. From crashing waves to extreme tides, surviving here takes ingenuity and incredible adaptations

programme.title: Kingdom of the Tide
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at the intertidal zone, the point at which the ocean meets the land, and the struggle for survival facing the creatures that inhabit this realm. Their success is critical, because this is one of the most important habitats on the planet - a vital connection between worlds

programme.title: Wild Rockies
programme.desc: A look at the lakes, rivers and marshes of the Rockies, which flow through the landscape and feed the creatures that call the mountains home

programme.title: Wild Rockies
programme.desc: A look at how survival in the forest means maintaining a delicate balance between growth and destruction - something the elk, moose and wolves know only too well

programme.title: Invisible River: Under Water Between the Black Forest and the Vosges
programme.desc: A documentary about the largest groundwater reservoir in Europe, which forms an underground river in the Upper Rhine Valley. Underwater cameraman Serge Dumont capture footage of the inhabitants of this biotope

programme.title: Great Parks of Africa
programme.desc: Cape Town's Table Mountain National Park, a rugged peninsula that juts out into the freezing Atlantic Ocean

programme.title: Nature's Dress Code: Who Wears What and Why?
programme.desc: Every living creature, from the amoeba to the blue whale, needs a suitable coat. Dive deep into the fashion styles of the natural world with this colourful documentary

programme.title: Weird, Wild and Wonderful
programme.desc: The natural world is filled with colourful, quaint and quirky characters. It may look crazy, but there are good reasons life got this way. This documentary reveals how, when the name of the game is survival, nothing is too outrageous

programme.title: The Ocean's Greatest Feast
programme.desc: The sardine run along the east coast of South Africa, a challenging voyage for the fish as they navigate the ocean, barely escaping the hungry predators who depend on them for food

programme.title: Shark with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Naturalist and shark expert Steve Backshall celebrates the dazzling diversity of sharks, uncovering their secret lives and the threats they face. He begins by exploring the radical variety of sharks in the Atlantic Ocean and marks the start of a global journey in the so-called shark capital of the world, the Bahamas

programme.title: Shark with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist learns about the sharks of the Indian Ocean, which despite finding ways to adapt to the environment, face threats from climate change, pollution and overfishing

programme.title: Shark with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist joins scientists to find the shark hotspots in the Pacific Ocean, where their precious locations are under threat and numbers are plummeting. Steve learns what the sharks offer that is so important to the Pacific and what can be done to preserve them

programme.title: Shark: British Isles Special with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Naturalist Steve Backshall goes in search of some of the 30-plus shark species that inhabit waters of the British Isles, showing how the big fish in home waters are just as astonishing and mysterious as their global cousins. Along the way he encounters a four-ton basking shark near the Hebrides and dives with his favourite of all time, the blue shark, off the coast of Cornwall. He also meets scientists and conservationists in Wales who are working to protect endangered species

programme.title: Wild Animal Babies
programme.desc: Patrick Aryee examines the first days, weeks and months of young animals, beginning by looking at how single offspring depend on their mothers

programme.title: Wild Animal Babies
programme.desc: Patrick Aryee explores how some siblings quickly forge lifelong bonds, while others compete ferociously from the moment they're born

programme.title: Wild Animal Babies
programme.desc: For animals born in bulk, life is a battle from the very start. Patrick Aryee marvels at the strategies nature has come up with to raise the next generation

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Elephant
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough investigates the life of Jumbo, a famous Victorian elephant, supposedly the largest that ever lived. Jumbo arrived at London Zoo in 1865 and was popular with the royal family, but was dangerously unhappy in captivity. In later life, he would be sold to PT Barnum's circus, becoming a celebrity before dying under mysterious circumstances. Attenborough examines Jumbo's remains and considers the lives of elephants in the wild to learn more about the animal's difficult life

programme.title: David Attenborough's Kingdom of Plants
programme.desc: The naturalist and film-maker explores the many varieties of plant life housed at the Royal Botanic Gardens in Kew, west London, and explains what they reveal about the natural world and the development of life on Earth. He begins inside the establishment's Palm House, whose climate has been engineered to simulate conditions found in rainforests, charting the evolution of plants that grow in the world's wettest regions and investigating how flowering plants spread across the globe so rapidly

programme.title: David Attenborough's Kingdom of Plants
programme.desc: The naturalist explores how the plants at Kew Gardens change through the seasons and demonstrates the surprising properties of several species, including the predatory pond-dwelling bladderwort utricularia and a fungus that appears to be capable of mind control. He also discusses the largest single organism on the planet, visits the world's biggest flower and explains how plants and insects can communicate using scents and music

programme.title: David Attenborough's Kingdom of Plants
programme.desc: The naturalist concludes his exploration of the Royal Botanic Gardens in Kew, west London, by examining plants that have evolved to minimise their dependence on water, enabling them to survive in the world's driest environments. He witnesses a cactus blooming at midnight, tells the story of the spectacular flowering of the Agave franzosinii, and visits the Millennium Seed Bank, a facility designed to save plants from future extinction

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: Stu and Liv catch a pregnant red-bellied black snake at a busy resort, while Mick and Adele face a lace monitor lizard

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: Stu and Liv outwit a blue tongue lizard, Jess and Dan free a trapped snake, and Mick and Adele rush a python rescue fast

programme.title: World's Most Toxic Animals
programme.desc: First of two programmes about some of earth's most dangerous creatures, which lurk in the oceans, the forests, swamps and deserts. In Australia, meet the world's most venomous snake, and in nearby oceans, discover the deadly nature of the marine creatures that share these tropical waters

programme.title: World's Most Toxic Animals
programme.desc: Exploring more of earth's dangerous creatures, beginning in the South American rainforest with the poison-packed golden dart frog, which harbours enough poison to kill 10 men. Plus, a visit to Sydney, Australia, home of the world's most venomous spider

programme.title: Wild Castles
programme.desc: The flocks of birds, families of wild boar and armies of insects that thrive in and around the centuries-old French fortress of Carcassonne

programme.title: Great Parks of Africa
programme.desc: Cape Town's Table Mountain National Park, a rugged peninsula that juts out into the freezing Atlantic Ocean

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Cameras follow four different species of baboons who have conquered African deserts, coasts, savannah and mountains, adapting to the challenges of their environment along the way

programme.desc: Stu and Liv outwit a blue tongue lizard, Jess and Dan free a trapped snake, and Mick and Adele rush a python rescue fast

programme.title: Boss Shark
programme.desc: Specialists study a school of great whites in the waters off New Zealand's Stewart Island in the hope of finding an elusive and intimidating alpha shark

programme.title: Boss Shark: Battle of the Sexes

programme.title: Big Cats: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Part one of two. Biologist Patrick Aryee explores how cats have evolved throughout the millennia, learning to thrive in inhospitable environments, as well as becoming the world's most popular pet. In the first edition there is spectacular footage of caracals jumping three metres to catch flying birds, and a rare Bornean bay cat, which was thought to have been extinct. Patrick finds out how tigers made the transition from South-east Asia's steamy jungles to the -40C temperatures of Siberia, and meets 'lion whisperer' Kevin Richardson

Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure, with Robin Williams, Dustin Hoffman, Julia Roberts, Bob Hoskins and Maggie Smith

programme.title: Back to the Future Part III (1990)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly is transported back to the days of the Wild West, where he strives to save his eccentric scientist friend Doc Brown from death at the hands of a villainous gunslinger. Final instalment in the sci-fi comedy trilogy, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Mary Steenburgen and Thomas F Wilson

programme.title: Back to the Future (1985)
programme.desc: Teenager Marty McFly travels back to the 1950s in a time machine built by his eccentric inventor friend. The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mother's heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover

programme.title: Jack the Giant Slayer (2013)
programme.desc: A farm boy accidentally rebuilds the link between the human world and a kingdom of man-eating giants in the sky, resulting in a princess being trapped in the monsters' realm. He joins a band of the king's knights on a rescue mission, but one of the group intends to betray them in a bid to seize the throne. Fantasy adventure, starring Nicholas Hoult and Ewan McGregor

programme.title: The Golden Compass (2007)
programme.desc: Orphan Lyra Belacqua lives in a parallel universe populated by magical and menacing characters. When her best friend becomes the latest in a long line of children to mysteriously vanish, she embarks on a perilous mission to uncover the dark secret behind their disappearance. Fantasy adventure based on Philip Pullman's novel Northern Lights, starring Dakota Blue Richards, Nicole Kidman, Daniel Craig and Eva Green. Featuring the voices of Ian McKellen, Kristin Scott Thomas and Kathy Bates

programme.title: Smurfs: The Lost Village (2017)
programme.desc: A mysterious map sends Smurfette, Brainy, Clumsy and Hefty on a quest into the Forbidden Forest, leading to the discovery of the biggest secret in Smurf history. Animated adventure, with the voices of Demi Lovato, Julia Roberts, Rainn Wilson and Joe Manganiello

programme.title: Wicked (2024)
programme.desc: Green-skinned Elphaba Thropp becomes part of the new intake at Shiz University, and shares a room with fellow spell-caster Galinda Upland. It's the beginning of a friendship which culminates in an epic showdown as Elphaba transforms a renegade witch with a skill for bursting into show-stopping songs, like Defying Gravity. Part one of The Wizard of Oz prequel based on the hit stage musical, starring Cynthia Erivo, Ariana Grande-Butera, Jonathan Bailey, Michelle Yeoh and Jeff Goldblum

programme.title: Smurfs (2025)
programme.desc: When Papa Smurf is taken by evil wizards Razamel and Gargamel, Smurfette leads her fellow blue creatures on a mission to the real world to save him. Live-action animated musical fantasy comedy, featuring the voices of Rihanna, James Corden and Nick Offerman

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania (2012)
programme.desc: Dracula opens a luxury resort for monsters, to provide a refuge from terrified humans. His cosy sanctuary is disrupted when a normal man unwittingly checks in, and the situation is complicated when the unwanted guest takes a liking to the count's daughter. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: The Golden Compass (2007)

programme.title: The Wizard of Oz (1939)
programme.desc: Youngster Dorothy Gale is whisked over the rainbow by a tornado into the magical land of Oz, where she inadvertently kills a wicked witch - incurring the wrath of another. Setting out to find the wizard who can show her a way home, she is joined on her journey by a scarecrow, a tin man and a cowardly lion, who all hope their own wishes will be granted. Musical fantasy, with Judy Garland, Ray Bolger, Jack Haley, Bert Lahr and Frank Morgan

programme.title: The Witches (1990)
programme.desc: A boy goes on holiday with his grandmother to a seaside hotel hosting a convention of witches, who have come together to hear their leader's plan to turn all the children in England into mice. When the chief hag discovers the young lad eavesdropping at their meeting, she decides to make him one of her first victims. Children's fantasy directed by Nicolas Roeg, based on the book by Roald Dahl, and starring Anjelica Huston, Mai Zetterling, Jasen Fisher, Jane Horrocks, Rowan Atkinson and Brenda Blethyn

After 100 years, the chosen one is found and must find a way to restore harmony. Fantasy adventure based on the cartoon series, starring Noah Ringer and Dev Patel.

programme.desc: A teenager picks up a distress signal originating from the middle of the ocean. Joining an expedition to track the signal to its source, he discovers an unexplored island inhabited by strange creatures, and searches for a way to rescue its sole human inhabitant. Fantasy adventure sequel to Journey to the Centre of the Earth, starring Dwayne Johnson, Michael Caine and Josh Hutcherson

programme.title: Robin and the Hoods (2024)
programme.desc: For the tenacious 11-year-old Robin and her loyal band of friends, the Hoods, the patch of overgrown scrubland at the end of their cul-de-sac is a magical kingdom. However, when the slippery property developer Clipboard dazzles the parents of the area with promises of health spas and day-care facilities, the gang risk losing their kingdom forever. Director Phil Hawkins' comedy, starring Naomie Harris, Darcey Ewart, Gwendoline Christie and Mark Williams

programme.desc: Officer Nolan tries to reunite a family, while Officer Harper's past resurfaces and threatens to jeopardise an overnight visit with her daughter

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann NicAmhlaoibh visit Galicia accompanied by acclaimed musicians Angus MacKenzie from Skye and multi-instrumentalist Barry Kerr from Dublin. During their trip to northern Spain they explore the city of Santiago de Compostela and take in the music and culture of the region, performing with renowned Spanish musicians including the influential singer Uxía, world-famous pipers Anxo Lorenzo and Susana Seivane and acclaimed Xabier Díaz and Xosé Liz. Julie performs with the folk-singing group Leilía and both presenters visit the famous Mercado de Abastos

programme.desc: Gloomy new tenant Mr Gray moves into Rigsby's boarding house, but proves so depressed that he barely seems to notice the dingy surroundings, and keeps talking about 'going to a better place' - raising fears he is contemplating suicide. programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Tired of getting nowhere with Miss Jones, Rigsby switches his amorous attentions to new lodger Marilyn, who turns out to be an exotic dancer - complete with python. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan starts dating a posh new girlfriend, but makes the mistake of asking Rigsby for advice on how to conduct himself in polite company - a sure-fire recipe for disaster. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Osborne seemingly dies after eating a bowl of Miss Jones' home-made soup, plunging the household into mourning - but Rigsby is the one who ends up being measured for a coffin. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby lets a room to a young couple he believes have just got married - unaware that they are not man and wife. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby decides to express repentance for his numerous sins, at the urging of his latest lodger, a religious student. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Don Warrington and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby imagines he is rich after being left a sizeable sum in his uncle's will. But there is a condition attached - and the landlord has to seek Miss Jones's help if he has any hope of seeing the money. Andrew Sachs guest stars, with Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: In a bid to find true love, Rigsby puts a personal advertisement in the local paper and receives an unexpected reply. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Ambrose bamboozles Rigsby by claiming to be a mystic, and soon has the other lodgers under a strange spell. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.sub-title: Come on in the Water's Lovely
programme.desc: Rigsby's divorce finally comes through, and he wastes no time in proposing to Miss Jones - but makes the mistake of asking his low-life brother Ron to be best man. Final episode of Eric Chappell's classic comedy, which finally reveals Rigsby's Christian name. Leonard Rossiter stars

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: As the comedy returns, there's bad news for the Stubbs family as they hear that Tracey's ex-husband has died and she has to decide what kind of send-off to give him. Sharon wants it to be nice for the boys, Chigwell's criminal fraternity are keen to make it special, while Dorien seems more interested in just finding the right outfit. Linda Robson, Pauline Quirke and Lesley Joseph star, with appearances from Spandau Ballet's Martin Kemp and one-time EastEnder Jamie Foreman (Derek Branning)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The family are devastated when unscrupulous burglars take advantage of their absence at Darryl's funeral to ransack the house. But rather than involve the Old Bill, they decide to track down their possessions themselves, setting out on a trail that takes them from a snooker parlour and a pawnbroker's to an infamous local drag club. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon disapproves of Tracey dating policeman Barry, especially as she appears to be neglecting Garth's feelings about his dear, departed dad, leading to a major argument between the sisters. Can Dorien solve their problems in her new role as a magazine agony aunt? Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A couple of the birds are forced to fly this week when one of them becomes fed up of being treated like a doormat. It seems Tracey has had enough of visits from Dorien's gentleman callers to her home, and is equally unhappy that her sister doesn't contribute to the household finances, so she throws them both out. The pair of them are soon holed up together in Sharon's old bedsit, where they decide to take a united stand - but things are never quite that straightforward. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.sub-title: Tonight I'll Be Staying Here with You
programme.desc: The lifts in Auntie Vera's block of flats are playing up, which is no good with her hips, so she announces to Sharon and Tracey that she is moving in with them. There's just one little snag - the only available room is Dorien's - and she is somewhat dismayed to be told that family comes first. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph, with a guest appearance from Kate Williams (Love Thy Neighbour)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey is very welcoming when a surprise visitor turns up in the form of the attractive Emma, although Sharon is suspicious of the newcomer and Dorien is shocked to the core. Travis, on the other hand, is over the moon. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Charlie Quirke

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Travis has serious school news that he is scared to break to his mum, so Sharon decides to pass herself off as Tracey and sort out his problems. However, her efforts only land the family in more trouble and it is left to Dorien to help out - using her skills of seduction. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Charlie Quirke

programme.title: Steptoe and Son
programme.desc: Christmas special from 1973. Harold decides to spend the festive season in Majorca and promptly heads off to the travel agent - then faces the grim task of telling Albert he'll be alone for the holidays. When his plans fall through, he decides to throw a party at home instead. Starring Harry H Corbett, Wilfrid Brambell and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Steptoe and Son
programme.desc: Christmas special from 1974. Ever the optimist, Harold again plans to spend Christmas abroad, but doesn't count on Albert's intransigence. In predictable fashion, the old man takes umbrage at the thought of being left behind in Shepherd's Bush, and insists on inviting himself along. Harry H Corbett and Wilfrid Brambell star in the last ever episode of the sitcom

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Mateo reveals his home life is a disaster - his mother-in-law Dolores is threatening to kill him - so Lesley urges the barman to move out and start a new life. But will it be that easy? Clive, Tiger and Terri come up with a plan to retrieve Clive's 5,000 euros from dodgy property developer Monty, while Psychic Sue drops by Blow & Go with a message for Liam. Jacqueline and Glynn look forward to a reunion with their real partners, but when Riannon arrives, there's no sign of Donald. Guest starring Ruth Madoc (Hi-de-Hi!)

programme.title: Shameless
programme.desc: Shane is consumed with guilt following the hit-and-run, and tries to make amends by befriending Ruth, the widow of the man he killed, but her teenage son takes an instant dislike to him.
